metadata.title: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
metadata.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Experimental study with hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
metadata.demographics: Ten male and ten female subjects, age 18-35 years, Caucasian race, BMI 20-25 kg/m², healthy lean individuals

evidence.quote: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
TheJournalofClinicalEndocrinology&Metabolism92(9):3646–3652
Maarten R. Soeters, Hans P. Sauerwein, Johanna E. Groener, Johannes M. Aerts, Mariëtte T. Ackermans, Jan F. C. Glatz, Eric Fliers, and Mireille J. Serlie
Copyright©2007byTheEndocrineSociety

Tenmaleand10femalesubjectswererecruitedviaadvertisements in local magazines. Criteria for inclusion were 1) absence of a family history of diabetes; 2) age 18–35 yr; 3) Caucasian race; 4) body mass index(BMI)20–25kg/m2
MainOutcomeMeasuresandDesign:Aftera38-hfast,measurements ofglucoseandlipidfluxesandmuscleceramideandfattyacid translocase/CD36wereperformedbeforeandafterahyperinsuline miceuglycemicclamp.
Ten male and 10 female subjects were recruited via advertisements in local magazines.
Criteria for inclusion were 1) absence of a family history of diabetes; 2) age 18–35 yr
body mass index (BMI) 20–25 kg/m2
the subjects were fasting from 2000h 2d before the start of the study until the end of the study
[6,6-2H]glucose (prime, 8.8 μmol/kg; continuous, 0.11 μmol/kg·min)
infusions of insulin (60mU/m2·min) (Actrapid 100 IU/ml; NovoNordisk FarmaB.V., Alphen aan den Rijn, The Netherlands)
glucose 20% (to maintain a plasma glucose level of 5 mmol/liter)
Plasma glucose concentrations were measured with the glucose oxidase method using a Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2 (Beckman, Palo Alto, CA) with intraassay variation of 2–3%.

methods_participants.label: Ceramide and glucosylceramide measurement method
methods_participants.method: Pro-Mag I biopsy needle under local anesthesia

evidence.quote: Muscle biopsies were performed to assess muscle content of ceramide, glucosylceramide, and FAT/CD36 at the end of both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp.

methods_participants.label: Pre-study diet
methods_participants.value: Weight-maintaining diet with at least 250g carbohydrates per day for 3 days

evidence.quote: For 3 d before the fasting period, all volunteers consumed a weight-maintaining diet containing at least 250 g carbohydrates per day.

methods_participants.label: Exercise Inclusion Criteria
methods_participants.value: Fewer than three times per week

evidence.quote: 5) no excessive sport activities, i.e. fewer than three times per week

narrative_results.statement: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

evidence.quote: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes

evidence.quote: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes
Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake during the clamp (Rd) did not differ significantly between men and women

narrative_results.statement: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp

evidence.quote: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp
There were no differences in muscle FAT/CD36 content between women and men during both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

evidence.quote: We suggest that during fasting, women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Cortisol was measured with a chemiluminescent immunoassay with intraassay variation of 7–8%, interassay variation of 7–8%, and detection limit of 50 nmol/liter.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Plasma glucose concentrations were measured with the glucose oxidase method using a Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2 (Beckman, Palo Alto, CA) with intraassay variation of 2–3%.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Glucagon was determined with the Linco 125I RIA (Linco Research, St. Charles, MO) with an intraassay variation of 9–10%, interassay variation of 5–7%, and detection limit of 15 ng/liter.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Plasma FFA concentrations were determined with an enzymatic colorimetric method (NEFA-C test kit; Wako Chemicals GmbH, Neuss, Germany)

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Ceramide and glucosylceramide in muscle biopsies were measured with an HPLC method as described (33). Muscle biopsies were weighed and homogenized in 300 μl water by sonification, and 50 μl muscle homogenates was used.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Insulin was measured with a chemiluminescent immunometric assay with intraassay variation of 3–6%, interassay variation of 4–6%, and detection limit of 15 pmol/liter.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Body composition was measured with bioelectrical impedance analysis (Maltron BF906; Maltron, Rayleigh, UK).
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Cortisol was measured with a chemiluminescent immunoassay with intraassay variation of 7–8%, interassay variation of 7–8%, and detection limit of 50 nmol/liter.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Women were studied during the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Glucagon was determined with the Linco 125I RIA (Linco Research, St. Charles, MO) with an intraassay variation of 9–10%, interassay variation of 5–7%, and detection limit of 15 ng/liter.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: For 3 d before the fasting period, all volunteers consumed a weight-maintaining diet containing at least 250 g carbohydrates per day.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Adiponectin was determined by a RIA (Linco) with intraassay variation of 2–7%, interassay variation of 16–17%, and detection limit of 1 ng/ml.

semantic_groups.rationale: Identical age inclusion criteria of 18-35 years
Identical BMI inclusion criteria of 20-25 kg/m²
Both report 38 hours fasting duration; pass_1 includes additional timing details (2000h 2d before start) while pass_2 is more concise

Both describe [6,6-2H]Glucose tracer with primed continuous infusion; pass_2 specifies prime dose of 8.8 μmol/kg while pass_1 lists units without specific dose value. Subscript notation differs (2H vs 2H₂) but refers to same compound

Both describe [1,1,2,3,3-2H5]glycerol tracer with stable isotope infusion; pass_2 specifies prime dose of 1.6 μmol/kg while pass_1 lists units without specific dose. Subscript notation differs (2H5 vs 2H₅) but refers to same compound
Identical insulin infusion rate of 60 mU/m²·min during hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
Pass_1 contains plasma glucose target information (5 mmol/liter) that is absent in pass_2
Both describe blood sampling at 2 hours after tracer infusion start during equilibration phase; pass_1 specifies 3 samples at T=2h while pass_2 describes timing context with fasting duration
Both describe blood sampling at 5-minute intervals at T=8h for glucose and glycerol enrichment via arterial blood draw. Minor wording variations in phrasing.
Identical facts: glucose oxidase method using Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2. Only difference is 'Plasma glucose' vs 'Glucose' in descriptor.
Same measurement method and kit. 'Free fatty acid' vs 'FFA' is standard abbreviation variation.
Identical timing and procedure details. Only minor wording difference in 'at end of' vs 'end of'.
Both specify final 20 minutes of basal state and clamp using ventilated hood system. Pass_2 adds 'hyperinsulinemic euglycemic' which clarifies but doesn't contradict.
Same assay method and instrument. 'Insulin assay' vs 'Insulin measurement' is negligible wording difference.
Identical method and instrument. 'Cortisol assay' vs 'Cortisol measurement' is negligible wording difference.
Both describe RIA method using Linco 125I RIA. Pass_1 specifies '125I RIA' while Pass_2 abbreviates to 'RIA', but both refer to same technique.

Both describe HPLC method for ceramide/glucosylceramide measurement. Pass 1 specifies 'muscle homogenate analysis' while Pass 2 just says 'HPLC' - same underlying method, different specificity levels.
Identical information about body composition measurement using bioelectrical impedance analysis with Maltron BF906 device.
Identical information about women's menstrual cycle phase being follicular phase.

Same pre-study diet requirement with identical values (250g carbohydrates per day for 3 days). Minor wording difference: 'Pre-study diet' vs 'Pre-study diet requirement' and 'per day' vs 'per day' notation.
Same participant information (10 male and 10 female subjects). Pass 1 labels it 'Participant N' while Pass 2 labels it 'Participant N and gender' - same fact, slightly different descriptor.
Pass 1 is empty/null while Pass 2 contains information about [6,6-2H₂]Glucose tracer dosing. This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 is empty/null while Pass 2 contains information about [1,1,2,3,3-2H₅]Glycerol tracer dosing. This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 is empty/null while Pass 2 contains information about plasma glucose measurement frequency. This information is only in Pass 2.
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains adiponectin measurement method information
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains FAT/CD36 measurement method information
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains statistical analysis method information
Identical statements about basal plasma FFA being significantly higher in women compared with men
Identical statements about insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake showing no sex difference
Identical statements about no gender difference in basal muscle ceramide
Identical statements about ceramide not correlating with peripheral glucose uptake
Identical statements about muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 showing no sex difference in basal state and during clamp
Identical statements about plasma epinephrine being significantly lower in females during hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
Identical statements about adiponectin being significantly higher in females in basal state and during clamp
Pass 1 contains statement about women having higher lipolysis rates in basal state but not during clamp; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains a finding about women's protection from FFA-induced insulin resistance; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains a finding about REE differences between men and women; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains a finding about glucose and fat oxidation rates; Pass 2 is empty
Both extractions contain identical text about plasma glucose levels being significantly lower in women than men
